_comment: Secret-shaped fixture values embed the __X__ defang splitter (stripped by score.py before scanning) so no contiguous secret-like literal exists in the repo. Labeled benchmark for the regex detector engine. Every value here is fake/synthetic/reserved — no real PII. surface: prose (natural mention), edge (boundary formatting), near-miss (values that look like PII but must be preserved, or realistic distractors alongside a real hit), injection (adversarial instruction-like text wrapped around real PII, proving regex can't be talked out of matching), leakage (realistic accidental-leak scenarios: configs, logs, tickets). gold: values the regex engine MUST catch (scored by type, see score.py). preserve: values that MUST NOT be flagged, with the type that would be a false positive if it appeared. expected_miss: true means every gold item in this case is outside regex's reach (e.g. person names) and is excluded from the recall calculation — it exists to document the boundary honestly, not to inflate the score.
